rationale: Apache-2.0, self-hostable 4B VLM for structured extraction from PDFs/invoices/forms hits practitioners immediately via HF Space + weights — directly relevant to FS document processing under data-residency constraints.
Concrete vLLM 0.8 routing pattern—MoE for easy steps, Opus for hard—cuts agentic coding costs 90% with 93% success; practitioners can replicate the architecture this week.

BeeLlama v0.2.0 delivers 4-5x inference speedup on a single RTX 3090 for Qwen3 27B and Gemma4 31B — directly actionable for engineers running local LLM inference with a GitHub repo and quickstart guides.

Nemotron-Labs diffusion LMs on HF offer a concrete inference-speed alternative to autoregressive decoding — practitioners can benchmark today, but production readiness and FS-specific applicability remain unproven.

Rigorous CPU-only benchmark shows 26M specialist model beats 0.6B generalist on function-calling accuracy and speed — directly actionable for engineers deploying tool-use agents on constrained or air-gapped hardware.
Apex-Testing offers a real-world agentic coding benchmark on 65-70 private repos — directly usable by engineers evaluating which model to deploy in coding-agent workflows this week.
Community fine-tune adds diarization+timestamps to the top open-source ASR model (Cohere Transcribe); HF release means engineers can adopt immediately for meeting/call transcription pipelines.

Safe Bilevel Delegation proposes a quantified trust-handoff mechanism for multi-agent systems—conceptually relevant to agentic FS workflows but single-source newsletter coverage with no repo, code, or paper link to act on.
